rulings.Case Title: PAUL RYAN ASSOCIATES, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. 44 NORMANDIE, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel 44 Normandie To Produce Docs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, February 3, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF PAUL RYAN ASSOCIATES, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION'S Motion To Compel 44 Normandie To Produce Documents Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear to discuss applicability of discovery referee's order of 1/16/14 which was made after this motion was filed. (Ex. U to Dooley dec.) It appears that all issues were resolved except for what documents needed to be produced in conjunction with the deposition of nonretained expert Michael Blakemore. (Id. p. 3.) It appears that Mr. Blakemore has yet to be deposed in an expert capacity. Apart from the foregoing, it appears that any issues regarding the production ordered by January 26th are not the subject of this motion, but may have bearing on it to the extent that documents at issue now have been produced. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: PAK HING YU VS. HIN DUN KONG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, February 3, 2014, Line 2, CROSS DEFENDANT ZHONG LUN WU's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Sustained with 10 days leave to amend pursuant to CCP § 430.10(e). The First Amended Cross-Complaint ("FACC") does not allege the elements of fraud with the requisite specificity. (See Lazar v. Superior Court (1996) 12 Cal.4th 631.) The FACC does not allege there was a misrepresentation, given that the alleged misrepresentation is that "Yu's electrician license was needed for electrical work on Hin Dun Kong's house," and yet the following sentence states that the license was in fact needed because Wu signed a contract with Kong using that license. (First Amended Cross-Complaint, 10.) It is also unclear how Wu misrepresenting to Yu that he needed his electrician license caused Yu to be sued for unlicensed construction. (Id.) The FACC also does not state facts to support a cause of action for equitable indemnity, because the FACC does not allege facts that give rise to tortious conduct on Cross-Defendant's part. (See Bailey v. Safeway, Inc. (2011) 199 Cal.App.4th 206, 217.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: DEIDRENNE ASHCRAFT VS. ROE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, February 3, 2014, Line 5, DEFENDANT EVENT MAGIC, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication. The motion for summary judgment is denied. The motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issue 1 (the premises liability cause of action). The motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues two and three. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether defendant's light tree caused plaintiff's injury. The trier of fact will determine how the light tree fell over. In addition, the Court denies as to issue 3 because (1) it appears to be an attempt to adjudicate a factual issue under the guise of duty in contravention of CCP 437c(f)(1); and (2) defendant failed to maintain its initial burden of production. The Court admits the declaration of Nichole West and defendant fails to present substantial evidence that plaintiff willfully concealed the witness. See Biles v. Exxon Mobil Corp., (2004) 124 Cal.App.4th 1315, 1324-1325. The Court denies defendant's request for a continuance. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: HENRY WOLF VS. BMW OF NORTH AMERICA, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn For Leave To Augment Expert Disclosures
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, February 3, 2014, Line 7, PLAINTIFF HENRY WOLF's Motion For Leave To Augment Expert Disclosures. Denied. Plaintiff has not made a sufficient showing to warrant leave to augment expert disclosure. Plaintiff has not made an adequate showing that the original disclosure was the result of "mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect." (CCP § 2034.620(b).) Plaintiff does not contend that Dr. McIntosh or Dr. Rutnich is unavailable, nor does he contend that they have flipped on previously expressed opinions. Rather, it appears that Plaintiff has simply decided, after the fact, that he would have taken a different approach to experts than the approach taken by former counsel. As Plaintiff's current counsel states in his declaration, he "would have done things much, much differently" than former counsel. (Martin Decl., 7.) This does not make the original disclosure the result of "mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect." (CCP § 2034.620(b).) The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: SEMEYON GOFMAN VS. WHITTIER ENTERPRISE LLC, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Attorney Fees As Prevailing Party
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, February 3, 2014, Line 15, PLAINTIFF SEMEYON GOFMAN'S Motion For Attorney Fees As Prevailing Party. Granted. Plaintiff is the prevailing party under CCP sec. 1032. Therefore, Plaintiff is entitled to recover attorney's fees provided for by statute under CCP sec. 1033.5(a)(10). Pursuant to Doran v. North State Grocery ((2006) 137 Cal.App.4th 484), Plaintiff is not entitled to recover attorney's fees under the Labor Code because the plain language of the statutes requires a determination of liability. However, the plain language of Govt. Code sec. 12965(b) does not require such a showing. Under Govt. Code sec. 12965(b), the Court is given discretion to award reasonable attorney's fees to the prevailing party. Defendant shall pay Plaintiff reasonable costs and attorney's fees in the amount of $5,000. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO COMMUNITY COLLEGE DISTRICT, A VS. HUNT CONSTRUCTION GROUP, INC., AN INDIANA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, February 3, 2014, Line 18, DEFENDANT MKTHINK's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Overruled. The certificate of merit was timely filed per CCP sec. 411.35 and CCP sec. 12(a). The complaint adequately alleges facts to state a cause of action for negligence and professional negligence against Defendant MKThink. (See Cooper v. Jevne (1976) 56 Cal.App.3d 860, 869, "the architects' duty of reasonable care in the performance of their professional services is logically owed to those who purchased the allegedly defectively designed and built [building].") Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
